commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Vercya Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Vercya's 4G13, 4G15 and 4D68 engines dominate UK replacement demand for this rare Taiwanese import van. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your commercial van.

problems.h4: Vercya 4G13 Timing Chain Wear - Why Early Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattle on cold start and potential valve timing drift.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain assembly, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents catastrophic valve damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 4G13 Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old engine, fit rebuilt 4G13 with upgraded timing components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage commercial vans; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi Vercya worth £3,000-£6,000, preventative chain replacement makes sense below 80k miles. Above that, a rebuilt 4G13 with upgraded components offers better long-term value for commercial operators.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G13 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Vercya

problems.h4: Vercya 4G13 Head Gasket Failure - Coolant Loss on High-Mileage Units
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping or gasket degradation from overheating or age causes coolant/oil mixing and compression loss.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Skim & Gasket Replace - Machine head, replace gasket set
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, replace gasket set, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block is undamaged; risk of hidden cracks

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 4G13 Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new head gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old engine, fit rebuilt 4G13 with upgraded gasket set, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for commercial vans; eliminates risk of residual damage

problems.recommendation: Head gasket failure often indicates broader cooling system neglect. For a work van, a rebuilt 4G13 with warranty is safer than risking a second failure after a head repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13 rebuilt engine with warranty

problems.h4: Vercya 4G15 VVT System Failure - Cam Phaser Issues on Later Petrol Engines
problems.rootCause: VVT cam phaser oil passages clog or mechanism wears, causing timing errors, rattle, and potential valve interference.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - VVT Solenoid & Oil Flush - Clean passages, replace solenoid
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove VVT solenoid, flush oil galleries, replace filter
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses early symptoms; not viable if phaser is mechanically worn

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 4G15V Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded VVT components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old engine, fit rebuilt 4G15V with upgraded VVT system, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for commercial reliability; warranty covers VVT-specific failures

problems.recommendation: VVT failures are complex to diagnose. If rattle persists after solenoid replacement, a rebuilt 4G15V with upgraded VVT components offers peace of mind for import van owners.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G15V engine prices for your Mitsubishi Vercya

problems.h4: Vercya 4D68 EGR Cooler Cracking - Diesel Emissions System Failure
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling cracks the EGR cooler housing, causing coolant loss, white smoke, and potential engine overheating.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Cooler Replacement - Replace cooler, flush cooling system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove EGR assembly, replace cooler, refill coolant, test for leaks
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if no secondary damage; coolant contamination risk remains

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 4D68 Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt diesel with EGR delete option
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old engine, fit rebuilt 4D68 with EGR modification option, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for commercial operators; eliminates recurring EGR issues

problems.recommendation: EGR cooler failure often precedes injector or turbo issues on commercial diesels. For a work van, a rebuilt 4D68 with EGR modification offers better uptime and lower long-term costs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Vercya 4D68 diesel replacement engine

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Vercya
finalCta.paragraph: All four engine families fitted to the Mitsubishi Vercya have known failure patterns that drive replacement demand among UK import van owners. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Vercya specialists who understand Taiwanese import vehicles and CMC Veryca cross-compatibility. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus nationwide delivery and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices and get your commercial van back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Vercya Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Vercya and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Mitsubishi Vercya 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L 4G13 was the base petrol engine for early Mitsubishi Vercya imports (2008-2012), offering simple MPI fuel injection and 87-94hp output; its widespread use in commercial applications means timing chain wear is the dominant replacement driver for UK owners.
commonFailurePoints: head gasket failure at high mileage

items.title: Mitsubishi Vercya 1.5L Petrol Engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Vercya 1.8L Petrol Engines

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Vercya Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Vercya's production run (2008-2012), organised so UK import van owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Mitsubishi Vercya launched with the 1.3L 4G13 petrol engine as the base powertrain for Taiwanese market commercial vans.
keyChanges: Initial Vercya production began with 4G13 1.3L MPI petrol engine
Commercial van body style introduced for urban delivery use
CMC Veryca platform sharing established for parts compatibility
knownFor: Simple MPI design aids maintenance for commercial operators
Timing chain tensioner wear emerges as high-mileage concern
Strong cross-compatibility with CMC Veryca parts network
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Mitsubishi Vercya replacement engines
years.ticker: Vercya 4G13 timing chain noise • Vercya 1.3L engine replacement UK • CMC Veryca compatible engine

years.description: The 1.5L 4G15 with VVT technology was introduced, offering improved power and efficiency for later Vercya variants.
keyChanges: Power increased to 99-105hp depending on configuration
ECU updates required for VVT system compatibility
knownFor: VVT system improves fuel economy but adds complexity
Cam phaser failures on 4G15V drive specialist replacement demand
Cross-reference with CMC Veryca 1.5L variants essential for UK owners
years.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Vercya 2010 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Vercya 4G15 VVT failure • Vercya 1.5L engine cost UK • 4G15V vs 4G15S difference

years.description: The 2.5L 4D68 diesel was introduced for commercial delivery applications, alongside the rare 1.8L 4G93 performance variant.
keyChanges: 4D68 2.5L DI-D diesel engine added for commercial van spec
Rare 4G93 1.8L petrol offered in select regional variants
EGR emissions systems introduced on diesel variants
knownFor: 4D68 EGR cooler cracking becomes dominant diesel failure
4G93 sought by enthusiasts but limited UK availability
Commercial operators prioritise supply & fit for minimal downtime
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011 Mitsubishi Vercya diesel and petrol engines
years.ticker: Vercya 4D68 EGR failure • Vercya diesel engine replacement • Vercya 1.8L rare engine

years.description: Final year of Vercya production; UK import owners now rely on specialist networks for engine replacement and CMC Veryca cross-compatibility.
keyChanges: Production concluded; focus shifts to aftermarket support
UK specialist suppliers establish rebuilt engine inventory for import vans
CMC Veryca parts cross-reference becomes critical for sourcing
knownFor: High-mileage 4G13 units drive timing chain replacement demand
Commercial operators seek warranty-backed rebuilt units for uptime
Taiwanese import specialists become key sourcing channel for UK owners

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Vercya Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Vercya the same as the CMC Veryca?
items.answer: Yes, the Mitsubishi Vercya is essentially a badge-engineered variant of the CMC Veryca from Taiwan. Engines and most parts are interchangeable, which is critical for UK owners sourcing replacements.
keyPoints: Vercya and Veryca share identical 4G13/4G15/4D68 engine families
Parts cross-reference simplifies sourcing for UK import owners
CMC Veryca donor vehicles can supply compatible replacement engines
items.cta: Compare CMC Veryca compatible engine prices for your Mitsubishi Vercya

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 4G13 1.3L petrol is generally reliable with proper maintenance, but timing chain tensioner wear and head gasket failure are common on high-mileage commercial use above 80k miles.
keyPoints: Simple MPI design aids maintenance and repair accessibility
Timing chain replacement recommended preventatively at 60-80k miles
Rebuilt 4G13 units with upgraded tensioners offer best commercial value
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13 engine with upgraded timing components

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Vercya engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Vercya engines typically cost £1,500-£2,200 for the 4G13 1.3L petrol, £1,700-£2,500 for the 4G15 1.5L, and £2,000-£2,800 for the 4D68 2.5L diesel, supply only.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine code, mileage, and supplier location
Supply & fit service adds £400-£800 labour depending on workshop
12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units through EngineMarket network
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi Vercya engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Vercya engine in the UK?
items.answer: UK availability is limited to specialist import suppliers and JDM breaker networks; EngineMarket connects you with vetted specialists who source 4G13/4G15/4G93/4D68 engines for Taiwanese import vans.
keyPoints: Main dealers do not stock Vercya engines as model was never UK-official
Specialist import suppliers maintain rebuilt inventory for commercial vans
CMC Veryca cross-compatibility expands potential donor vehicle pool
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Vercya engine specialists via our UK comparison tool

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13 engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, the 4G13 can reach 150,000+ miles, but commercial van use and short-journey operation often accelerate wear on timing components and head gaskets.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes and timing component inspection extend lifespan
Cooling system maintenance critical to prevent head gasket failure
Rebuilt units with upgraded components can match or exceed original longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for preventative maintenance or rebuilt Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13 engine

items.question: When should I change the timing chain on a Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13?
items.answer: Preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement is recommended at 60,000-80,000 miles on the 4G13, or immediately if cold-start rattle is detected.
keyPoints: Listen for rattle on cold start as early warning sign of tensioner wear
Replacement cost (£500-£900 specialist) is far less than valve damage repair
Rebuilt engines include upgraded timing components as standard
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Vercya 4G13 timing chain replacement vs rebuilt engine costs

items.question: Can I import a Mitsubishi Vercya engine from Taiwan?
items.answer: Yes, but import costs, shipping, and potential ECU compatibility issues often make UK specialist-sourced rebuilt engines more practical and cost-effective for commercial operators.
keyPoints: Import duties, shipping and VAT can add £300-£600 to base engine cost
UK rebuilt engines include warranty and local support for commercial uptime
CMC Veryca cross-reference expands UK donor vehicle availability
items.cta: Compare UK-sourced Mitsubishi Vercya engine prices before considering import

items.question: Where is the engine code located on a Mitsubishi Vercya?
items.answer: The engine code (4G13/4G15/4G93/4D68) is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, and also appears on the vehicle's VIN plate and registration documents for imported vans.
keyPoints: Check engine block stamping for definitive code identification
Cross-reference with CMC Veryca documentation if Vercya badging is unclear
Registration lookup via EngineMarket instantly verifies engine code and variant
items.cta: Enter your registration to instantly identify your Mitsubishi Vercya engine code

items.question: Does a rebuilt Mitsubishi Vercya engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all rebuilt engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, covering parts and labour for commercial peace of mind.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary slightly by supplier; confirm coverage before purchase
Rebuilt units with upgraded components often qualify for extended warranty options
Supply & fit service typically includes labour warranty alongside engine coverage
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Vercya rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty included

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Mitsubishi Vercya van?
items.answer: For commercial operators, a rebuilt engine (£1,500-£2,800) often offers better value than purchasing a replacement import van, especially when factoring in warranty coverage and minimal downtime with supply & fit service.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost is typically 30-50% of a replacement import van price
12-month warranty protects against early failure, reducing commercial risk
Supply & fit service minimises downtime for revenue-generating vehicles
